title: bun HTMLRewriter double-free: the end()-error path finalizes a Response the JS wrapper already owns (PR #30196)
category: use-after-free / double-free (object ownership across the Zig core ↔ JSC wrapper boundary)
crash_site: src/bun.js/webcore/Response.zig::finalize and the GC-time JSResponse destructor (codegen/ZigGeneratedClasses) — where the double-free is OBSERVED on a LATER GC sweep, NOT where it originates

ground_truth.decoy_fn: Response.finalize / the JSResponse GC destructor (the crash site); also the sibling write() error path in the SAME sink, which handles ownership CORRECTLY
ground_truth.fix_summary: The buffered output sink's end() error branch called response.finalize() (and set sink.response = undefined) on a Response already owned by its JS wrapper cell created via sink.response.toJS() in init(); a later GC sweep then double-freed it through the JSResponse destructor. Fix: drop the manual finalize in the end() catch and let the JS wrapper own the lifetime — matching the already-correct write() error path.

admission.note: monogram+haiku & baseline+haiku DISCRIMINATION round. Expected HARD + discriminating (unlike ksmbd-37899, which baseline-haiku solved 1/1 = non-discriminating / training-contaminated). The ASAN trace points at Response.finalize and the GC JSResponse destructor (the symptom); the cause is the synchronous end() error path of the buffered output sink double-finalizing a JS-owned Response — a cross-boundary OWNERSHIP bug with no text bridge from crash site to cause. Two live decoys at base: (1) the crash-site finalize/destructor, (2) the symmetric write() error path which is CORRECT. Obscure 2026 fuzzing find → low training-data contamination.

provenance: oven-sh/bun PR #30196 'Fix HTMLRewriter use-after-free when handler rejects during end()' (merged 2026-05-03; squash-merge 0561f87d42ce; pre-fix base d0a0bc4c9a5d). Flaky SIGSEGV found by fuzzing; ASAN use-after-poison reached via the GC sweep of the Response JS wrapper (JSRef.deinit → Response.finalize → JSResponse::~JSResponse). Repro: an HTMLRewriter document/element handler returns a rejected promise on the final lastInTextNode chunk during transform() of an ArrayBuffer/string, then Bun.gc(true). Single-concern fix (html_rewriter.zig +1/-3) → clean grading.
